mission.title: Install and Configure Open Policy Agent (OPA)
mission.description: OPA is a CNCF graduated general-purpose policy engine that enables unified, context-aware policy enforcement across the cloud-native stack. It uses the Rego policy language for writing and evaluating policies.

steps.title: Install OPA binary
steps.description:
Download and install the OPA binary.

```bash
# macOS
brew install opa

# Linux
curl -L -o opa https://openpolicyagent.org/downloads/v1.15.2/opa_linux_amd64_static
chmod 755 opa
sudo mv opa /usr/local/bin/
```

steps.title: Verify installation
steps.description:
Check OPA is installed correctly.

```bash
opa version
```

steps.title: Run OPA as a server
steps.description:
Start OPA as a policy decision service.

```bash
opa run --server &
curl localhost:8181/v1/data
```

> OPA listens on port 8181 and responds to policy queries via REST API.

steps.title: Test with a sample policy
steps.description:
Create and evaluate a simple Rego policy.

```bash
echo 'package example
default allow := false
allow if input.user == "admin"' > policy.rego
echo '{"user": "admin"}' | opa eval -d policy.rego -i /dev/stdin 'data.example.allow'
```
commands:
echo 'package example
default allow := false
allow if input.user == "admin"' > policy.rego

uninstall.title: Uninstall OPA
uninstall.description:
Remove OPA.

```bash
# macOS
brew uninstall opa

# Linux
sudo rm /usr/local/bin/opa
```
